text: Working with APIs allows different software applications to communicate with each other. When you make an HTTP request to an API, you typically receive data in JSON format, which is easy for programs to read. Many APIs require authentication, often using an API key, to ensure only authorized users can access the data. It is also important to be aware of rate limits, which restrict how many requests you can make in a certain time period. Finally, good error handling is essential—you should always check the response status and manage problems like network failures or invalid requests gracefully.
